red_state.summary: Focused desktop authoring tests compile and execute, then fail for missing phase-local recommended skill selector and missing recommended skill reference round-trip preservation.

failed_tests.test: WorkflowTemplateEditor > lets authors select recommended skills per phase from the shared skill catalog
failed_tests.reason: Unable to find an accessible group named /recommended skills for plan phase/i; current editor still exposes no shared-catalog phase-local recommended skill selector.

failed_tests.test: WorkflowTemplateEditor > round-trips legacy reason and unknown skill reference fields when saving recommended skills
failed_tests.reason: Saved phase skills preserve legacy name/reason only; mode, source, pluginName, referenceId, contentHash, and unknown fields are dropped.

validation_output.command: cd desktop && bun run test -- --run src/components/workflow/WorkflowComponents.test.tsx src/__tests__/skillsSettings.test.tsx
validation_output.summary: WorkflowComponents.test.tsx: 38 tests, 2 failed. skillsSettings.test.tsx: all added/existing tests passed in the focused run. Failures are behavior gaps, not syntax or harness errors.

concerns: The new workflow selector test assumes the implementation will expose a semantic group labelled by active phase, e.g. 'Recommended skills for Plan phase', with add buttons for catalog skills.

The skills settings catalog test currently verifies source/provenance visibility and absence of bundled contents through existing SkillList behavior; deeper workflow-specific reuse will be covered by the editor selector implementation.
recovery_hints: Implement WorkflowTemplateEditor phase-local recommended skill selection from useSkillStore or the shared catalog/API surface, without fetching or rendering skill file contents when selecting.
Serialize selected phase skills as references with mode: 'recommended' and preserve legacy reason plus unknown fields from existing template skill entries.
Keep pluginName as provenance metadata on plugin-provided skills; do not convert phase bindings into plugin-primary objects or mandatory gates.
